pulse: Global markets surged to record highs on May 6 as US-Iran ceasefire talks advanced, crushing oil prices and igniting a broad risk-on rally. The S&P 500 closed at a new all-time high above 7,365, AMD soared 18% on AI earnings, and the Kospi hit a record for the eighth time this year. The dominant macro question now is whether a Hormuz deal materialises and whether the incoming Warsh Fed tightens into any peace dividend.

news.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Advance â€” Hormuz Remains Effective Chokepoint
news.text: Trump paused Project Freedom military escort operations in the Strait of Hormuz to allow diplomatic space for a deal brokered through Pakistani intermediaries. Iran is reviewing a US proposal including a 30-day window to resolve nuclear demands and unfreeze assets. About 1,600 ships remain stranded. Brent eased to around $108 on the news but remains 50% above pre-war levels.

news.title: S&P 500 Hits Record 7,365 on Iran Peace Hopes and AI Earnings
news.text: The S&P 500 closed at an all-time high of 7,365.12 on May 6, up 1.46%, powered by AMD's blowout AI earnings and rising hopes for an Iran ceasefire. The Dow crossed 49,900 and Nasdaq Composite surged over 2%. Q1 2026 earnings season is tracking an 84% beat rate with record 13.4% net profit margins.

news.title: AMD Soars 18% on AI Chip Demand; Broad Semiconductor Rally
news.text: Advanced Micro Devices beat Q1 2026 on revenue and EPS, citing surging server CPU demand for AI workloads, and issued strong Q2 guidance. The result sparked a broad chipmaker rally and reinforced the AI-driven tech trade powering US equities in 2026.

news.title: Kospi Hits All-Time Record â€” Up 75% Year-to-Date â€” as Samsung Briefly Tops $1 Trillion
news.text: South Korea's Kospi surged 6.5% on May 6 to a record high, its eighth single-day gain of over 5% in 2026, taking the year-to-date advance to 75%. Samsung Electronics jumped around 13% as its market cap briefly crossed $1 trillion for the first time, driven by AI semiconductor demand.

news.title: Fed Holds at 3.50 to 3.75%; Powell Exits May 15 as Warsh Advances
news.text: The Federal Reserve held rates steady at its April 29 meeting, Powell's last as chair. Kevin Warsh was confirmed by the Senate Banking Committee 13 to 11 and is set to assume the chairmanship on May 15. Traders are pricing increasing odds that Warsh hikes before cutting, as inflation runs at 3.3% and rate-cut odds for 2026 sit at just 12%.

news.title: US Threatens 25% Auto Tariffs on EU Unless Trade Deal Ratified
news.text: Washington warned Brussels it would impose 25% tariffs on European vehicles relatively soon if the EU fails to ratify the July 2025 trade agreement. European finance ministers threatened retaliation; German automakers urged de-escalation.

news.title: Somali Piracy Surges as Naval Patrols Diverted to Hormuz
news.text: Three ships including an oil tanker and two merchant vessels were hijacked off Somalia in two weeks to May 7. Naval forces previously patrolling the Gulf of Aden have been redeployed to Hormuz operations, creating a security vacuum being exploited by Somali pirates.

news.title: Spirit Airlines Ceases Operations Amid Sustained Fuel Price Shock
news.text: Spirit Airlines shut down following surging jet fuel prices and a failed federal bailout, with no remaining path out of bankruptcy. The collapse highlights how sustained energy price shocks from the Iran war are reshaping corporate viability in fuel-intensive sectors.

news.title: IMF Downgrades Global Outlook; Central Banks Out of Good Options
news.text: The IMF April 2026 World Economic Outlook, titled Global Economy Tested Again, flagged central banks burning foreign reserves to defend currencies amid dollar strength and energy inflation. Emerging market sovereign spreads have widened materially.

news.title: IBEX35 Up 2.46%; Endesa Profits Surge 24% in Q1 2026
news.text: The Spanish benchmark closed at 17,668 on May 6, up 2.46%, boosted by Middle East de-escalation hopes and strong corporate earnings. Endesa posted Q1 net profit of 725 million euros, up 24% year-on-year, beating analyst estimates.

news.title: Escribano Family Sells 14.3% Indra Stake for 1.3 Billion Euros
news.text: The Escribano brothers divested their entire stake in defence-tech company Indra through an accelerated book-building, netting around 900 million euros in capital gains. One of Spain's largest equity placements of 2026, the sale raises questions about Indra's shareholder structure amid rising European defence spending.

news.title: Catalonia 2026 Budget Up 22.8%; 4.1 Billion Euro Investment Plan Approved
news.text: The Catalan government approved a 2026 budget 22.8% larger than the last approved budget in 2023, with 4.1 billion euros earmarked for public investment targeting infrastructure, housing, and social welfare.

news.title: Singapore STI Crosses 5,000; MAS Tightened in April on Inflation Risks
news.text: Singapore's Straits Times Index crossed 5,000 for the first time in 2026, delivering a 29% price return over 12 months. MAS increased the rate of appreciation of the S$NEER band in April, citing Iran-war-driven energy inflation, even as Q1 GDP contracted 0.3% quarter-on-quarter on a seasonally adjusted basis.

news.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7; Signs SpaceX Computing Deal
news.text: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.7 with advanced software engineering capabilities and improved vision, signed a deal with SpaceX to access 300-plus megawatts of compute at the Colossus 1 data centre in Memphis, and launched pre-built AI agent suites for major global banks with Microsoft Office integrations.

news.title: OpenAI to Spend $50 Billion on Compute in 2026; Launches GPT-5.5 Instant
news.text: OpenAI confirmed $50 billion in 2026 compute infrastructure investment and finalised a $10 billion joint venture with TPG, Brookfield, Advent, and Bain Capital for enterprise AI deployment. GPT-5.5 Instant was released for low-latency voice AI. Cloud distribution expanded beyond Microsoft to Amazon.

news.title: Google DeepMind Pentagon Deal Sparks First AI Lab Union Vote
news.text: Google signed a deal allowing the US Department of Defense to use Gemini AI models in classified military networks. UK-based DeepMind workers voted 98% in favour of forming a union with the CWU, what would be the world's first union at a frontier AI lab.

day_ahead: May 7 key events: US weekly jobless claims (consensus around 215K); potential Fed speaker commentary ahead of Warsh's May 15 start; US-Iran ceasefire talks â€” any official statement from Tehran or Washington will be a major market mover; European auto sector monitoring US tariff deadline; ECB June rate decision increasingly in focus.

ai_news.title: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 Released; SpaceX 300MW Compute Deal Signed
ai_news.text: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7 with substantially improved advanced software engineering capabilities and higher-resolution vision. The company signed a deal with SpaceX to access over 300 megawatts of compute from Colossus 1 in Memphis, and launched pre-built AI agent suites for global banks and Claude add-ins for Excel, PowerPoint, and Word.

ai_news.title: OpenAI: $50B Compute Spend, $10B PE Joint Venture, GPT-5.5 Instant Launch
ai_news.text: OpenAI confirmed $50 billion in 2026 compute infrastructure investment and finalised a $10 billion joint venture with TPG, Brookfield, Advent, and Bain Capital for enterprise AI deployment. GPT-5.5 Instant was released for low-latency voice AI, and cloud distribution expanded to Amazon with a Google tie-up in discussion.

ai_news.title: DeepMind Workers Vote 98% for Union After Pentagon AI Deal
ai_news.text: UK-based DeepMind workers voted 98% in favour of forming a union with the CWU, the first vote of this kind at a frontier AI lab, following Google's deal to deploy Gemini models in US classified military networks. The US Commerce Department CAISI also signed pre-deployment AI safety agreements with Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI.

ai_news.title: JPMorgan and Novo Nordisk Lead Enterprise AI Adoption Wave
ai_news.text: JPMorgan Chase reclassified AI as core infrastructure with a $19.8 billion tech budget and 2,000 dedicated AI staff, projecting $2.5 billion in annual AI-generated value. Novo Nordisk announced a partnership with OpenAI to integrate AI across drug discovery, clinical trials, and manufacturing, targeting full deployment by end of 2026.
